prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_regulatory_commitment site_level_commitment_1)
(create_regulatory_commitment product_level_commitment_1)
(create_regulatory_commitment commitment_package_1)
(create_regulatory_commitment commitment_package_2)
(assign_personnel_to_commitment site_level_commitment_1 personnel_resource_1)
(assign_personnel_to_commitment product_level_commitment_1 personnel_resource_2)
(assign_personnel_to_commitment commitment_package_1 personnel_resource_3)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(submit_commitment_for_approval site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(assign_approver_to_commitment site_level_commitment_1 functional_approver_1)
(initiate_site_impact_assessment site_level_commitment_1 impact_area_1 evidence_item_1)
(complete_site_investigation_with_approver site_level_commitment_1 impact_area_1 functional_approver_1)
(unassign_approver_from_commitment site_level_commitment_1 functional_approver_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment product_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(submit_commitment_for_approval product_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(assign_approver_to_commitment product_level_commitment_1 functional_approver_1)
(initiate_product_impact_assessment product_level_commitment_1 regulatory_region_1 evidence_item_1)
(complete_product_investigation_with_approver product_level_commitment_1 regulatory_region_1 functional_approver_1)
(unassign_approver_from_commitment product_level_commitment_1 functional_approver_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment product_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(assemble_submission_package site_level_commitment_1 product_level_commitment_1 impact_area_1 regulatory_region_1 submission_record_1)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(perform_submission_quality_check submission_record_1 site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment commitment_package_1 evidence_item_1)
(submit_commitment_for_approval commitment_package_1 evidence_item_1)
(assign_quality_document_to_submission commitment_package_1 quality_document_1 submission_record_1)
(perform_package_precheck commitment_package_1 quality_document_1 submission_record_1 evidence_item_1)
(assign_risk_assessment_to_package commitment_package_1 risk_assessment_document_1)
(assign_validation_protocol_to_package commitment_package_1 validation_protocol_1)
(assign_approver_to_commitment commitment_package_1 functional_approver_1)
(apply_risk_assessment_to_package commitment_package_1 risk_assessment_document_1 functional_approver_1 quality_document_1 submission_record_1)
(start_detailed_review_without_validation_or_risk commitment_package_1 validation_protocol_1 quality_document_1 submission_record_1)
(finalize_package_simple commitment_package_1)
(finalize_commitment_and_link_regulatory_reference commitment_package_1 regulatory_reference_1 evidence_item_1)
(close_package_and_release_resource commitment_package_1 personnel_resource_3 regulatory_reference_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment commitment_package_1 evidence_item_1)
(unassign_approver_from_commitment commitment_package_1 functional_approver_1)
(unassign_risk_assessment_from_package commitment_package_1 risk_assessment_document_1)
(unassign_validation_protocol_from_package commitment_package_1 validation_protocol_1)
(assign_personnel_to_commitment commitment_package_2 personnel_resource_3)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment commitment_package_2 evidence_item_1)
(submit_commitment_for_approval commitment_package_2 evidence_item_1)
(assign_quality_document_to_submission commitment_package_2 quality_document_2 submission_record_1)
(perform_package_precheck commitment_package_2 quality_document_2 submission_record_1 evidence_item_1)
(assign_risk_assessment_to_package commitment_package_2 risk_assessment_document_1)
(assign_validation_protocol_to_package commitment_package_2 validation_protocol_1)
(assign_approver_to_commitment commitment_package_2 functional_approver_1)
(apply_risk_assessment_to_package commitment_package_2 risk_assessment_document_1 functional_approver_1 quality_document_2 submission_record_1)
(start_detailed_review_without_validation_or_risk commitment_package_2 validation_protocol_1 quality_document_2 submission_record_1)
(finalize_package_simple commitment_package_2)
(finalize_commitment_and_link_regulatory_reference commitment_package_2 regulatory_reference_1 evidence_item_1)
(close_package_and_release_resource commitment_package_2 personnel_resource_3 regulatory_reference_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment commitment_package_2 evidence_item_1)
(register_site_commitment_from_package site_level_commitment_1 submission_record_1)
(register_product_commitment_from_package product_level_commitment_1 submission_record_1)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(finalize_commitment_and_link_regulatory_reference site_level_commitment_1 regulatory_reference_1 evidence_item_1)
(close_site_commitment_and_release_resource site_level_commitment_1 personnel_resource_1 regulatory_reference_1)
(detach_evidence_from_commitment site_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(attach_evidence_to_commitment product_level_commitment_1 evidence_item_1)
(finalize_commitment_and_link_regulatory_reference product_level_commitment_1 regulatory_reference_1 evidence_item_1)
(close_product_commitment_and_release_resource product_level_commitment_1 personnel_resource_2 regulatory_reference_1)
```

## Explanation of Generated Plan

The provided plan is a valid sequence of 65 grounded PDDL actions that transforms the initial state to a state satisfying the goal. It systematically processes the site-level commitment, product-level commitment, and both commitment packages through their respective workflows, reusing limited resources (e.g., evidence_item_1, functional_approver_1, regulatory_reference_1, risk_assessment_document_1, validation_protocol_1, personnel_resource_3) via attach/detach, assign/unassign mechanisms. Key phases include:

- **Entity Creation and Personnel Assignment**: All four goal entities are created and assigned personnel (reusing personnel_resource_3 for packages).
- **Site and Product Processing**: Each undergoes creation, evidence attachment, approval submission, approver assignment, impact assessments, and completion to achieve `ready` and `investigation_completed` states.
- **Submission Assembly and Quality Check**: Uses the standard assembly variant leveraging assessed impacts/regions, followed by quality check with evidence reattached to site commitment.
- **Package Processing (for pkg1 and pkg2)**: Each package follows: evidence attachment/submission, quality document assignment to shared submission, precheck (leveraging no validation/risk flags), risk/validation assignments (reused), approver assignment, risk application, detailed review start (without path for simple finalization), simple finalization, linking, and closure.
- **Site/Product Registration**: Post-package closures, uses shared submission to set `submission_prepared`, then finalizes/links/closes each.

This plan avoids complex paths (e.g., sequenced approvals, external consultations) and confirms goal predicates (`entity_registered` for site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, commitment_package_1, commitment_package_2) without extraneous actions.
message.reasoning:
The problem involves tracking regulatory commitments in pharmaceutics. There are site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, and two commitment_packages: commitment_package_1 and commitment_package_2. The goal is to register all of them: site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, commitment_package_1, and commitment_package_2.


## Examining regulatory commitments  
- The task focuses on tracking various regulatory commitments in the pharmaceutical sector.  
- Key elements include site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, and two commitment packages: commitment_package_1 and commitment_package_2.  

## Planning registration process  
- The goal is to register all identified commitments: site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, commitment_package_1, and commitment_package_2.  
- Next steps involve organizing these commitments for efficient registration.

## Planning registration process  
- Each commitment (site_level_commitment_1, product_level_commitment_1, commitment_package_1, commitment_package_2) needs to be created using create_regulatory_commitment, as they aren't initialized yet.  
- Personnel (1, 2, 3) must be assigned to each entity, though availability may limit simultaneous assignments, with actions like close_site_commitment_and_release_resource releasing them later.  

## Checking entity finalization  
- To register, entities need to be finalized via finalize_commitment_and_link_regulatory_reference, requiring entity_submission_prepared, evidence_attached, and a regulatory reference (ref1 available).  
- Entity_submission_prepared for sites and products can come from register_site_commitment_from_package or register_product_commitment_from_package, needing readiness, completed investigations, and quality checks.  

## Analyzing package dependencies  
- Commitment packages (pkg1 with qd1, pkg2 with qd2) include site1 and prod1, suggesting shared dependencies, but sites and products must be registered once despite multiple packages.  
- External stakeholders (es1 available for pkg1, es2 unavailable for pkg2) may affect package processing, requiring separate handling for pkg2.  

## Exploring registration flow  
- Packages have their own finalization process (e.g., finalize_package_simple) to reach entity_submission_prepared, then link references and register.  
- The overall flow involves creating entities, assigning personnel, attaching evidence (evidence_item_1 available), and navigating complex interdependencies for registration.

## Exploring registration assembly  
- Different assembly variants (e.g., with_impacted, with_region_documentation, with_full_checks) set various includes flags like includes_val_prot or includes_risk_ass, depending on readiness and completion status.  
- Assembly requires a single submission_draft, suggesting only one assemble process, despite two packages sharing the same submission.  

## Examining entity dependencies  
- Sites and products need their own processes (create, assign personnel, etc.) to be ready and completed, even if included in packages.  
- Packages require entity_ready_for_approval, needing create, personnel assignment, evidence attachment, and submission for approval, with limited personnel (3) for 4 entities.  

## Assessing registration steps  
- To register site1 and prod1, use register_from_package to set submission_prepared, then finalize_and_link (needing evidence_attached) and close_and_release, requiring assigned personnel.  
- Packages follow similar steps: finalize_package_simple or complete_approver_sequence_and_finalize_package to reach submission_prepared, then finalize_and_link, and close_package_and_release_resource.

## Processing entity readiness  
- Sites and products (site1, prod1) need creation, personnel assignment (e.g., p1 to site1), and evidence attachment (e.g., e1) to reach ready_for_approval.  
- Approver assignment (e.g., fa1) and investigation completion (via initiate_assessment and complete_investigation) make entities site_ready and investigation_completed, sharing limited resources like fa1 and e1 via unassign/detach.  

## Reviewing package prerequisites  
- All package processing (e.g., start_detailed_review) requires package_validation_protocol_assigned, even for variants without validation or risk assessments.  
- Assigning quality documents to submissions needs entity_ready_for_approval for packages, requiring prior creation, personnel assignment, evidence attachment, and submission for approval, with only one evidence item available.  

## Outlining registration plan  
- First, process site1 and prod1 to be ready and investigation_completed using shared resources like fa1 and e1, managing personnel constraints.  
- Then, assemble the submission (e.g., standard variant) requiring site_ready, prod_ready, and impact assessments, followed by quality checks and package-specific steps.

## Managing evidence attachment  
- Evidence (e1) needs attaching to site1 for quality checking, allowing quality checks while keeping e1 linked.  
- Detaching e1 from site1 frees it for package (pkg1) use, enabling evidence attachment and submission for approval.  

## Assigning quality documents  
- Quality document (qd1) gets assigned to submission (sub1) after pkg1 reaches ready_approval, consuming qd1 and linking it to sub1.  
- This step ensures pkg1 meets prerequisites for further checks, with qd1 no longer available afterward.  

## Reviewing package readiness  
- Package precheck (perform_package_precheck) requires evidence attached, quality document consumed, and linked, marking pkg1 as reviewed.  
- Risk assessment (rad1) and validation protocol (vp1) are assigned to pkg1, consuming available resources and preparing for detailed review.  

## Finalizing package assessments  
- Assigning an approver (fa1) to pkg1 enables risk assessment application, marking pkg1 with quality and risk documents.  
- Starting detailed review is possible without validation or risk, given pkg1’s has_quality_and_risk_documents status and linked resources.

## Processing package registration  
- Package 2 (pkg2) completes risk assessment with quality document 2 (qd2) assigned, enabling detailed review without validation or risk.  
- Finalizing pkg2 involves linking regulatory reference 1 (ref1) and closing, making personnel resource 3 (p3) and ref1 available.

## Managing evidence for site and product  
- Evidence item 1 (e1) needs detaching from pkg2 to attach to site1, ensuring site1 can finalize with ref1 linked.  
- After finalizing and closing site1, e1 detaches for prod1, allowing prod1 to attach e1 and link ref1 for finalization.

## Registering site and product commitments  
- Site1 and prod1 registration requires evidence attachment and regulatory reference availability, using shared submission record 1 (sub1).  
- Both site1 and prod1 are prepared for finalization after attaching e1 and linking ref1, releasing resources upon closing.

## Completing product investigation  
- Product investigation for prod1 finishes with functional approver 1 (fa1) assigned, ensuring regulatory region 1 (reg1) compliance.  
- After completion, fa1 unassigns from prod1, freeing resources for other tasks.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
